facts: In 1492, Christopher Columbus sailed from Europe to the Americas on three ships: the Niña, the Pinta, and the Santa María.
Columbus called the lands he reached the 'New World' because Europeans did not know these lands existed before his voyage.
The three ships that Columbus used were powered by wind, not engines, because in 1492 ships were sailing ships.
misconceptions: Columbus sailed to the Americas with only one ship.
